{"query":"New Caledonia 2024 state of emergency lift date curfew end confirmation official timelines","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1766,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/5/16/state-of-emergency-takes-effect-in-new-caledonia-after-four-killed-in-riots","title":"‘Intolerable’: State of emergency in New Caledonia as unrest spreads | Politics News | Al Jazeera","content":"Three Indigenous Kanak people and a police officer have been killed in violence that erupted on Monday night and has continued despite an overnight curfew.
The state of emergency came into force at 5am on Thursday (18:00 GMT on Wednesday) and gives the authorities wide powers of search and arrest.
The state of emergency will remain in force for 12 days.
This week’s violence came as the National Assembly voted in Paris to adopt the measure."},{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/france/20240528-france-lifts-state-of-emergency-in-new-caledonia-curfew-maintained","title":"France lifts state of emergency in New Caledonia, maintains curfew - France 24","content":"May 28, 2024 — France on Tuesday lifted a state of emergency. Issued on: 28/05/2024 - 08:42. A night curfew from 6:00 pm to 6:00 am
While the archipelago has been quieter, some disturbances were reported in Vallee-du-Tir district of the main city Noumea.
The lifting of the blockades is "the necessary condition for the opening of concrete and serious negotiations", the French presidency said in a statement, which announced the lifting of the state of emergency from 5:00 am Tuesday (1800 GMT Monday).
President Emmanuel Macron flew to the Pacific archipelago on Thursday in a bid to defuse the crisis.
The first French tourists were evacuated Saturday from Noumea aboard military aircraft headed for Australia and New Zealand.
Australia and New Zealand had already begun repatriating their nationals on Tuesday."},{"url":"https://www.abc.net.au/news/2024-05-27/state-of-emergency-in-new-caledonia-to-be-lifted/103897404","title":"French government to lift New Caledonia's state of emergency in bid to ease tensions on riot-hit island - ABC News","content":"A state of emergency declared in New Caledonia following an outbreak of deadly riots is expected to end at 5am on Tuesday, local time, but an overnight curfew will remain for the time being.
France's Elysée Palace revealed the end of the state of emergency in a statement on Monday.
The voting rule was brought in under the 1998 Nouméa Accord in an attempt to give the Indigenous Kanak population more representation.
Mr Macron's decision not to renew the state of emergency declaration illustrated Paris' desire to start the process of de-escalation and re-establish conditions for dialogue, the statement from the Elysée Palace added.
On Saturday, it was announced an extra 480 gendarmes would be sent to New Caledonia, bringing the number of French security forces there to about 3,500.
New Caledonia's top French official, High Commissioner Louis Le Franc said on Monday that an evening and overnight curfew will remain in place.
New Caledonia's ban on public gatherings, transport and carrying of weapons and sale of alcohol will also remain in place, Mr Le Franc said.
Last Tuesday, more than 100 Australians and other tourists were evacuated on military transport planes from Nouméa."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/new-caledonia-state-of-emergency-comes-into-effect/a-69092596","title":"New Caledonia state of emergency comes into effect","content":""These events could have been avoided if the French government had listened," he said, adding that Paris should establish contact with the Kanak Socialist National Liberation Front (FLNKS) alliance of political parties which has organized the protests."},{"url":"https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20240528-new-caledonia-makes-a-slow-return-to-normal-as-state-of-emergency-lifted","title":"New Caledonia makes a slow return to normal as state of emergency lifted - RFI","content":"France on Tuesday lifted a state of emergency in its Pacific territory of New Caledonia but is maintaining a curfew and sending hundreds of paramilitary reinforcements after two weeks of deadly riots.
A night curfew from 6.00pm to 6.00am and a ban on alcohol sales will remain, the French government office in New Caledonia said."}]}
